heading: Approval of applications
label: 11213 Approval of applications
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section. Some section numbers or references in amendment notes below reflect the classification of such sections or references prior to editorial reclassification.section 5713 of Title 42
Prior ProvisionsA prior was classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to repeal by .section 313 of Pub. L. 93–415section 5712a of Title 42Pub. L. 102–586

Amendments1999— inserted section catchline and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “An application by a State, locality, or private entity for a grant under section 5711(a), (c), or (d) of this title may be approved by the Secretary only if it is consistent with the applicable provisions of section 5711(a), (c), or (d) of this title and meets the requirements set forth in . Priority shall be given to grants smaller than $200,000. In considering grant applications under , priority shall be given to organizations which have a demonstrated experience in the provision of service to runaway and homeless youth and their families.”Pub. L. 106–71section 5712 of this titlesection 5711(a) of this title1992—, substituted “section 5711(a), (c), or (d) of this title” for “” in two places in first sentence and substituted “$200,000” for “$150,000” in second sentence.Pub. L. 102–586, § 3(d)section 5711(a) of this title1988—, substituted “” for “this part” in three places.Pub. L. 100–690, § 7271(c)(1)section 5711(a) of this title1984— substituted “private entity” for “nonprofit private agency”.Pub. L. 98–4731980— substituted “$150,000” for “$100,000” and “organizations which have a demonstrated experience in the provision of service to runaway and homeless youth and their families” for “any applicant whose program budget is smaller than $150,000”.Pub. L. 96–5091977— substituted “$100,000” and “$150,000” for “$75,000” and “$100,000”, respectively.Pub. L. 95–115

children.text: the geographical distribution in such State of the proposed services under this part for which all grant applicants request approval; and
which areas of such State have the greatest need for such services.
eligible applicants who have demonstrated experience in providing services to runaway and homeless youth; and
eligible applicants that request grants of less than $200,000.
